LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PROFERIAN, LLC, JAMES TROUT MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE MOTION BEING REFILED IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 1005. THE MOTION WAS FILED ON FEBRUARY 17, 2017, WHICH IS INSUFFICIENT NOTICE FOR A MARCH 7, 2017 HEARING. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Civil Contempt
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF SUNWOO HWANG's Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Civil Contempt is GRANTED IN PART. Pursuant to CCP 664.6, the court awards judgment in favor of Hwang against defendants Proferian LLC and James Trout jointly and severally for $32,418.02 in attorney fees, $328.56 in interest, and additional attorney fees of $1,560 for filing the motion to enforce (Total judgment = $34,306.58).

The court DENIES Hwang's request for contempt and a $1,000 fine. Hwang fails to demonstrate willful disobedience of a lawful court order beyond a reasonable doubt. (See People v. Sup. Court. (1965) 239 Cal.App.2d 99, 104.)

The court awards fees for filing the motion to enforce because the parties' underlying agreement allowed the prevailing party to recover reasonable attorney's fees, the request for $1,560 in fees for the instant motion is reasonable, and there is little point in requiring Hwang to file a subsequent motion for fees, which would only generate more fees. It is up to the parties whether Hwang will enforce the judgment or whether they can work out a payment schedule.

ORDER and JUDGMENT were signed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SUNWOO HWANG's Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Expenses.

Plaintiff Sunwoo Hwang's motion for attorney's fees and expenses is granted in large part. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of fees of $28,721 and expenses of $3797.02 for a total award of $32,518.02 against defendants Proferian LLC and James Trout. The applicable statute for fees is Civil Code 1717, not 1717.5 because there is a fees clause in the MIPA. All of the hours spent by plaintiff's counsel were reasonable. Defendants do not contest any of the costs claimed in the memorandum of costs filed by plaintiff. Plaintiff does not provide a persuasive reason why he should receive a fees award in excess of what he has paid. The court has included $3000 in fees and $90 in expenses for this motion.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
